slides.title: Your bedroom may be *too bright* to sleep
slides.sub: Even faint ambient light can quietly disrupt the night your body uses to repair itself. Total darkness is a metabolic tool.

slides.title: Darkness is a signal
slides.body: Your body reads darkness as a cue to wind down. Even *dim ambient light* during sleep can blunt that signal.

slides.title: Light suppresses melatonin
slides.body: Melatonin guides you into deep, restorative sleep. Low overnight light *can suppress melatonin* and keep the body subtly alert.

slides.title: A busier night
slides.body: Sleeping with light is linked to a *higher overnight heart rate*. Your body works harder during the hours meant for rest.

slides.title: It reaches your metabolism
slides.body: One night of light during sleep is linked to *greater insulin resistance* the next morning, which may strain long-term metabolic health.

slides.title: Aim for true darkness
slides.body: Blackout curtains, covered LEDs and a dark room *can support* deeper sleep and steadier overnight metabolism.

slides.title: See how your nights *add up*
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, food, movement and steps, then turns them into one daily Health Score so you can protect the habits that matter.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The room you sleep in might be working against you. Even a little light at night can quietly disrupt your metabolism. 🌙😴

Darkness protects melatonin, a calmer overnight heart rate and steadier morning blood sugar. Blackout curtains and covered LEDs go a long way. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep, food and movement into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Total Darkness Protects Your Metabolism at Night
